content: Routing-quality slice: advanced the supplied port-routing demo integration beyond port assignment. routeEdges now builds initial routes with the demo-style routeSegment logic (outward normal stubs, orthogonal side-aware routing, channel records), resolves overlapping h/v channels, and reselects nearby source/target face+port candidates only when the default route would hit nodes, coincide with borders, or bend excessively. Existing safety passes remain in place for endpoint spacing, component-safe repair, and final stub validation. Dist smoke on core-architecture and local-first-ui shows zero edges with more than 4 points; focused routes like parser_registry now reduce to straight/short orthogonal paths. Verification: npx tsc --noEmit, npm test (227 passing), npm run build. Existing untracked reports/ was left untouched. Dev server is still listening on 5173. Next: visually smoke localhost on the user-highlighted Local-first/Web Frontend regions, then tune only remaining visible routing defects such as excessive parallel-lane closeness or overlay-specific labels.
